summary.why: Phase 6 makes the staleness window per-repo configurable. 24h is a good default per the tally, but agent-UX advocate's review flagged that long-running sessions could exceed it (orchestrator + subagent fanouts running 4-10h, all-day refactors). Without this override, those repos would silently stale-skip their own work. The directive is opt-in: repos that don't set it get 24h, which the tally confirmed is safe.
summary.how: Added LoadSessionWindow(root) as a separate pass over .timbersignore — isolated from loadSkipConfig to avoid threading a new return value through 10+ existing call sites (4-tuple → 5-tuple breaks every test that destructures it). The file is tiny so dual-parse cost is negligible. SessionWindowResult exposes Window (effective value with default fallback baked in) + Raw + ParseErr for doctor diagnostics. Updated classifyTimbersIgnoreLine to explicitly skip session-window: lines so they don't leak into the path-rule set. NewDefaultStorage now stacks LoadSessionWindow on top of LoadProvenanceConfig. Added checkSessionWindow doctor check that warns on malformed values with the documented grammar in the hint. 12 unit tests cover valid grammar, malformed inputs, comments, last-wins, missing file.

notes: Considered returning a struct from readTimbersIgnore as the unified output instead of growing the tuple — rejected because the existing parser is well-tested and structurally simple; the dual-pass is the smallest viable change. Considered making negative durations a parse error — rejected because Go's time.ParseDuration parses them as valid Duration values, and treating them as malformed would require diverging from the documented grammar. Settled on 'coerce to default on negative or zero' which is the safe-degradation contract. dupword linter flagged the 'session-window:\nsession-window:' test fixture as a duplicate; resolved with a //nolint:dupword comment because the duplication is intentional (last-wins test).
